focus: route generation and matrix algorithmic complexity

findings.title: Route-local accounting repeats snapshot scans and balance reads on the execution hot path
findings.why_it_matters: executeRoute snapshots every route asset, then each leg calls _routeLocalBalance for the input amount, the output baseline, and the output after swap. The leftover refund pass calls _routeLocalBalance again for every snapshot. Each route-local read linearly scans snapshots through _baseline and then performs an external balance read for ERC20 assets. With the 16-leg cap this remains bounded, but it can still add dozens of balanceOf/staticcall reads plus O(asset_count^2) memory scans to the user-paid router execution path.

evidence.detail: _executeLeg calls _routeLocalBalance for leg.tokenIn, leg.tokenOut before dispatch, and leg.tokenOut after dispatch.
_refundRouteLocalLeftovers loops over every snapshot and calls _routeLocalBalance for each entry.
_baseline linearly scans the full snapshot array.
ERC20 balances are read through staticcall balanceOf, so repeated route-local checks become repeated external calls.

findings.suggested_fix: Precompute compact asset indexes while building snapshots and carry per-leg tokenIn/tokenOut indexes into execution, so baseline lookup is O(1). In the refund loop, compute current balance minus snapshots[i].baseline directly instead of calling _baseline again. Keep the current fail-closed balance read semantics; measure generated split and max-leg route gas before and after any lower-level balanceOf helper change.

residual_risks: The TypeScript compiler and matrix generator use small static route and fixture sets today, so no generator-side performance finding met the threshold. If they become graph-search based over larger pool catalogs, revisit pool lookup indexing and matrix route matching.
testing_gaps: No gas benchmark was run in this review because the requested mutation scope was limited to the review artifact. Add or compare Foundry gas snapshots for generated split routes and a high-leg synthetic route before accepting the accounting optimization.
